verses.text: David lifts up his soul to God, trusting that none who wait on Him will be put to shame. He stakes his hope on God's faithfulness. Those who truly wait on the Lord are never finally disappointed.
David lifts up his soul to God, trusting that none who wait on Him will be put to shame. He stakes his hope on God's faithfulness. Those who truly wait on the Lord are never finally disappointed.
David lifts up his soul to God, trusting that none who wait on Him will be put to shame. He stakes his hope on God's faithfulness. Those who truly wait on the Lord are never finally disappointed.
'Shew me thy ways, O LORD; teach me thy paths,' he prays, waiting all the day. He longs to be led in God's truth. The humble heart asks not only for help but for guidance into the right way.
'Shew me thy ways, O LORD; teach me thy paths,' he prays, waiting all the day. He longs to be led in God's truth. The humble heart asks not only for help but for guidance into the right way.

He pleads God's ancient tender mercies and asks Him to 'remember not the sins of my youth.' He casts himself on grace, not merit. Forgiveness rests on God's mercy and His name, never on our worthiness.

He pleads God's ancient tender mercies and asks Him to 'remember not the sins of my youth.' He casts himself on grace, not merit. Forgiveness rests on God's mercy and His name, never on our worthiness.
'Good and upright is the LORD,' who teaches sinners the way and guides the meek in judgment. His goodness stoops to instruct the lowly. He delights to lead the humble who own their need.
'Good and upright is the LORD,' who teaches sinners the way and guides the meek in judgment. His goodness stoops to instruct the lowly. He delights to lead the humble who own their need.
'Good and upright is the LORD,' who teaches sinners the way and guides the meek in judgment. His goodness stoops to instruct the lowly. He delights to lead the humble who own their need.

'For thy name's sake... pardon mine iniquity; for it is great,' he prays, knowing God teaches all who fear Him. He pleads the very greatness of his sin. The bigger our guilt, the more it magnifies the mercy that pardons it.

'For thy name's sake... pardon mine iniquity; for it is great,' he prays, knowing God teaches all who fear Him. He pleads the very greatness of his sin. The bigger our guilt, the more it magnifies the mercy that pardons it.

'For thy name's sake... pardon mine iniquity; for it is great,' he prays, knowing God teaches all who fear Him. He pleads the very greatness of his sin. The bigger our guilt, the more it magnifies the mercy that pardons it.

'The secret of the LORD is with them that fear him,' and David keeps his eyes ever toward the LORD. Intimacy is the reward of reverence. Those who fear God are admitted into His friendship and counsel.

'The secret of the LORD is with them that fear him,' and David keeps his eyes ever toward the LORD. Intimacy is the reward of reverence. Those who fear God are admitted into His friendship and counsel.

He begs God to turn to him in loneliness and affliction, to see his troubles and 'forgive all my sins.' He brings both sorrow and guilt together. The same prayer asks for comfort in trouble and cleansing from sin.

He begs God to turn to him in loneliness and affliction, to see his troubles and 'forgive all my sins.' He brings both sorrow and guilt together. The same prayer asks for comfort in trouble and cleansing from sin.

He begs God to turn to him in loneliness and affliction, to see his troubles and 'forgive all my sins.' He brings both sorrow and guilt together. The same prayer asks for comfort in trouble and cleansing from sin.

He asks God to guard his soul, let integrity preserve him, and 'redeem Israel... out of all his troubles.' His prayer widens to all God's people. The believer's pleas reach beyond himself to the whole flock of God.
